100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On March 29, 1926, the League of Nations convened in Geneva, addressing global disarmament as tensions rose in Europe. In the United States, the nation was still buzzing from the recent Supreme Court decision that upheld the prohibition of alcohol, fueling ongoing debates about the effectiveness of the Volstead Act. Meanwhile, in Germany, the economic crisis deepened as inflation continued to spiral, prompting widespread public unrest.
Updated: Sunday, March 29, 2026 at 07:24:26 AM CDT
